name: heavy_payload_burst
description: Few messages but huge. Each m_workersQueue element is a std::vector<char> ~1 MB; a moderate backlog weighs several GB. Validates that OOM risk doesn't require 'many' messages — heavy ones are enough.
notes: Payload is inflated to 1 MB via load.payload_size_bytes ('_pad' field added to the DataValue JSON).
Hypothesis: 100 agents × 50 DataValue = 5000 messages/window. If the queue retains 5000 × 1 MB = 5 GB, RSS spikes even though throughput looks low.
Proxy: monitor_rss_mb.png + monitor_vms_mb.png — RSS peaks correlated with Start bursts, not with duration.
Expect end_ack_error if the indexer HTTP response returns 413 (inherited from the large_payload scenario).
